summary: gpt-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host identity and CI metadata to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js automatically sends an HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping carrying installer-side reconnaissance data: os.hostname, DNS-resolved FQDN, env HOSTNAME/HOST/COMPUTERNAME, USERDNSDOMAIN (Active Directory corporate domain), current working directory basename, Node version, npm_config_registry, and CI repo slugs (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE/GITLAB/BITBUCKET/AZURE/TRAVIS/JENKINS identifiers). The destination is a hardcoded bare IPv4 over plain HTTP, with no consent prompt and no opt-out. The package's library entry (index.js) exports `{}`, so the only behavior of installing this package is the recon beacon. The package name 'gpt-sdk' suggests an OpenAI/GPT SDK, increasing the chance a developer installs it expecting a working SDK and instead leaks internal hostnames, AD domain, and CI repository identifiers to the operator of 46.224.67.169. README framing the package as a 'security research honeypot' does not change the installer-side impact: non-consensual recon exfiltration of corporate identifiers fires on every install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
